title: Understanding the Problem
text: The ball is thrown horizontally from a height of $$100$$ meters. We need to find out how far it travels horizontally before hitting the ground.

title: Time to Hit the Ground
text: First, calculate the time it takes for the ball to hit the ground. Use the formula for the time of free fall: $$t=\sqrt{\frac{2h}{g}}$$, where $$h$$ is the height (100 m) and g is the acceleration due to gravity $$(9.8$$ m/s²).

title: Calculate Time of Fall
text: Calculate the time it takes for the ball to fall $$100$$ meters using the formula $$t=\sqrt{\frac{2h}{g}}$$.

title: Horizontal Distance Calculation
text: Now that you have the time, calculate the horizontal distance using the formula: $$distance=speed time$$. The initial horizontal speed is $$20$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$.

title: Calculate Horizontal Distance
text: Use the formula $$distance=speed time$$ with $$speed=20$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ and $$time=4.52$$ s to find the horizontal distance.

title: Final Adjustment
text: Consider the descent of the balloon. Since the balloon is descending at $$2$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, adjust the horizontal distance accordingly.

title: Adjust for Balloon Descent
text: Subtract the distance covered by the balloon's descent (2 $$\frac{m}{s}$$ * $$4.52$$ s) from the horizontal distance.
